name: Pho Rosebud
signature: Rosebud's go-to Vietnamese  -  big steaming bowls of pho, rice paper rolls, and the deeply satisfying antidote to a heavy winery lunch
editorNote:
Pho Rosebud is the counter-programming the Peninsula's big lunch culture needs. The room is utilitarian, the menu is honest Vietnamese  -  pho, rice paper rolls, lemongrass chicken, a tight set of classics  -  and the bowls arrive big enough to solve almost any post-cellar-door situation. This is the place you go on the evening after the winery lunch, when nothing else on the Peninsula map is appealing and what you actually want is clean broth, fresh herbs, and a bowl of something honest.

The service is quick, the prices are closer to Footscray than Main Ridge, and the room fills with locals in a way that tells you everything about where the place sits in the Rosebud rhythm.

Get the pho dac biet. Add the chilli. Order rice paper rolls for the table.
